preview_text:
The model rates Saudi Arabia as the slight favorite with a 40% win probability, while Cape Verde's chances stand at 29%. That margin reflects a competitive fixture in which the underdog remains a genuine contender rather than a long shot.

The draw emerges as the modal outcome in the model forecast at 31%, suggesting a relatively balanced contest. The three outcomes span only 12 percentage points, indicating neither team holds decisive edge. A draw would not surprise; neither would a narrow win for Saudi Arabia or an upset Cape Verde victory.

The model and sportsbooks diverge modestly on draw likelihood. The model assigns it 31%, while consensus across 37 books places it at 26%—a gap of roughly 5 percentage points. Polymarket aligns closely with the books on the Saudi Arabia win (40.5% vs 40.3% model) and Cape Verde win (33.5% vs 28.6% model). The dashboard makes no claim about which probability is more accurate; the discrepancies may reflect different risk models or sample composition among oddsmakers.
